deploy/charts/benthos-collector.purpose: Helm chart packaging the benthos-collector binary as a Kubernetes StatefulSet. Chart metadata + values live at the root; the templates/ child renders the actual K8s resources. Primary constraints: stable pod identity for leader election (StatefulSet), credentials protected in a Secret (not ConfigMap), and config-source exclusivity enforced by the benthos-collector.args helper.

patterns.name: Three mutually-exclusive config sources with fixed precedence
patterns.description: values.yaml exposes config > configFile > preset; precedence is resolved by the benthos-collector.args helper in templates/_helpers.tpl, never in values.yaml or statefulset.yaml.
patterns.example: config: {} # takes precedence over configFile and preset

patterns.name: Config rendered into a Secret, never a ConfigMap
patterns.description: The Benthos config.yaml is base64-encoded into templates/secret.yaml to protect embedded OpenMeter tokens/credentials; a parallel ConfigMap for the same data is forbidden.

patterns.name: All names via helper macros
patterns.description: Resource names come from benthos-collector.fullname / benthos-collector.componentName; never string-concat .Release.Name.

patterns.name: StatefulSet with leader-election + storage toggles
patterns.description: leaderElection.enabled (RBAC + lease config) and storage.enabled (PVC volumeClaimTemplates) gate optional features; both rely on the StatefulSet's stable pod identity.

key_file_guides.role: Chart metadata; version 0.0.0 and appVersion "latest" are placeholders rewritten at release time.
key_file_guides.watch_for: appVersion should align with the published benthos-collector image tag on release.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the three config sources (config/configFile/preset), openmeter url/token, leaderElection, storage (PVC), rbac, and standard pod knobs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: config precedence is enforced in the args helper, not here; storage uses a per-pod PVC requiring StatefulSet.

key_file_guides.role: README.md is generated from README.tmpl.md via helm-docs; README.tmpl.md only contains {{ template "chart.base" . }}.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Edit README.tmpl.md and values.yaml comments, never README.md directly.

anti_patterns: Storing the Benthos config.yaml in a ConfigMap instead of a Secret — leaks embedded credentials
Hardcoding release/chart name strings instead of the benthos-collector.fullname/componentName helpers
Bumping appVersion/version without aligning to the released image tag
Using a Deployment instead of StatefulSet — breaks leader election and per-pod PVCs

decisions.rationale: Leader election needs stable hostname-based pod identity, and volumeClaimTemplates needs StatefulSet for per-pod persistent storage when storage.enabled.

decisions.decision: Config stored in a Secret, not ConfigMap
decisions.rationale: The Benthos config can embed API tokens; Secret provides base64 encoding and RBAC access-control semantics ConfigMap lacks.

deploy/charts/openmeter.purpose: Helm chart deploying all OpenMeter binaries (api, balance-worker, billing-worker, sink-worker, notification-service) plus optional bundled dependencies (Kafka, ClickHouse, PostgreSQL, Redis, Svix) as Kubernetes workloads. Chart metadata/values live at the root; templates/ renders the workloads. Primary constraint: all binaries share one ConfigMap-mounted config.yaml and a single image tag to prevent version skew across the mono-repo image.

patterns.name: Single shared image + tag for all OpenMeter binaries
patterns.description: Every workload uses Values.image.repository and Values.image.tag (default .Chart.AppVersion); only Svix has its own Values.svix.image. Per-component image overrides are forbidden.

patterns.name: Optional dependencies gated by conditional subcharts + .enabled flags
patterns.description: Chart.yaml declares Bitnami Kafka/PostgreSQL/Redis and the Altinity ClickHouse operator as conditional dependencies (condition: <name>.enabled / clickhouse.operator.install); each must also be guarded in resource templates and configmap.yaml.

patterns.name: Single config passthrough overwritten by chart-derived values
patterns.description: Values.config is a passthrough merged in configmap.yaml; chart-derived connection strings (from enabled flags) overwrite matching keys at render time — documented in values.yaml comments.
patterns.example: # Values defined in `config` will get overwritten by the values calculated from chart values!

patterns.name: Per-component replicaCount
patterns.description: Each binary has its own <component>.replicaCount (api, balanceWorker, billingWorker, sinkWorker, notificationService); workloads otherwise stay homogeneous.

patterns.name: CronJobs for billing/sync with fixed schedules
patterns.description: values.yaml jobs.* (subscriptionSync, billingCollectInvoices, billingAdvanceInvoices) define cron schedules; the templates use concurrencyPolicy: Forbid to prevent duplicate billing runs.

patterns.name: Bitnami legacy image-repo overrides
patterns.description: Bitnami moved free images to bitnamilegacy/*; the kafka/redis/postgresql subchart blocks override every nested image.repository (kafka, kubectl, jmx-exporter, redis-sentinel, os-shell, etc.) to bitnamilegacy/*.

key_file_guides.role: Declares altinity-clickhouse-operator, Bitnami kafka/postgresql/redis as conditional subchart dependencies; version is the chart release version.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New subcharts must follow the .Values.<name>.enabled condition pattern and update Chart.lock.

key_file_guides.role: Per-component replicaCount, all dependency enable/disable toggles, job cron schedules, Svix config, bitnamilegacy image overrides, and the config passthrough block.
key_file_guides.watch_for: config is a passthrough — chart-derived values overwrite matching keys at render; warn users in comments when adding new overridable keys.

key_file_guides.role: Reference config showing meters, events/notification/entitlements feature flags.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Keep in sync with the config schema in app/config; it is documentation, not rendered.

key_file_guides.role: README.md is generated from README.tmpl.md via helm-docs (chart.baseHead + chart.valuesSection).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Edit README.tmpl.md and values.yaml comments, never README.md.

anti_patterns: Using different image fields per OpenMeter binary — all share Values.image; only Svix is exempt with Values.svix.image
Adding optional infra without both a {{- if .Values.<service>.enabled }} guard in the resource AND an override block in configmap.yaml
Hardcoding resource names with .Release.Name instead of openmeter.componentName/fullname helpers
Adding a Deployment/CronJob without a checksum/config annotation — config changes won't trigger rollouts
Pinning Bitnami images to docker.io/bitnami/* instead of bitnamilegacy/* — those tags are no longer published

decisions.decision: Single shared ConfigMap and image/tag for all workloads
decisions.rationale: All binaries ship in one mono-repo Docker image and read the same config.yaml; a shared ConfigMap + checksum annotation rolls all workloads on any config change and prevents version skew between components sharing compiled domain types.

decisions.decision: Bundled dependencies are conditional subcharts disabled for production
decisions.rationale: Kafka/ClickHouse/PostgreSQL/Redis/Svix are bundled for dev convenience but documented as not-for-production; operators disable them and provide connection details via config.
Duplicate concurrent billing or subscription-sync runs would cause double-billing or invoice-advancement races.
